ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-03-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-16-300442 Conservatorship of Philip Lam and
PTR-17-300976 The Philip Lam Special Needs Trust.

Appearances: Susanna Cohen for Karen Fisher (Conservator/Trustee).

Ms. Cohen addressed the Court's concerns stating that the ex parte petition to increase bond in the trust case was filed in March, that everything has been taken care of and that they are asking for Court approval of fees. Ms. Cohen also stated that the Trustee had an ongoing fee order in one matter, but not the other, that it was a misunderstanding, that now the investments are brought in compliance, that the Trustee viewed EFT's as domestic stock investments and that it is routinely done in Contra Costa County. In addition, Ms. Cohen stated that the Trustee wanted investments that would produce large returns, that the Trustee was not aware of the local rule, that Mr. Lam is doing extremely well because of the advocacy of her Client as well as hers (Ms. Cohen) in the conservatorship and that the majority of time spent was in the conservatorship. The Court stated that the Trustee should have been familiar with the local rules and mindful of what orders say and don't say. Ms. Cohen stated that she now knows that she will have to sit down with her Clients and go over the local rules, that she did not file anything regarding travel time, that she is willing to submit on the pleadings, that research was done regarding Laura's Law because they were asked to do so (oral communication) by the Court Investigator, that the task was to help a young man who was being evicted, that Mr. Lam was not taking his medications and that the task was to keep him off the streets and back on his medications. The Court questions why it should continue to allow fees on account given the Trustee's past actions. Ms. Cohen stated that the Trustee is now absolutely clear, that they have asked for fees on account in both the conservatorship and the trust, that the issue has been corrected, that they have filed for termination of the conservatorship and that they will modify their request to three hours a month which is reducing it from 5 hours. Ms. Cohen also clarified that the Conservator is not asking for additional fees of $990.00, that it is not a new payment, that $519.45 is for the conservator of the person, that there is no conservator of the estate and that $6,419.45 was paid on account to Ms. Fisher. The Court granted the Amended First Account and Report of Trustee, etc. and the Petition for Order to Approve and Pay Conservator's Fees, etc. (subject to review of the orders). The Court stated that it will modify the order, that it will take out the travel time and leave in the research time, and that it will allow fees on account, but the Conservator is to be mindful.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-16-2019 Continued To Sep-03-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Susanne Cohen for and with Karen Fisher (Trustee).

The Court noted that the conservatorship fees were taken without approval, that there is no petition in the conservatorship for fees, and that it is appropriate to refund them to the trust and ask for them in the conservatorship. The Court also noted that more trustee's fees were taken than what was allowed on account, that it was suggested in the papers that trustee time was 21 plus hours, that ten hours (2 five hour invoices) had no description and that the request for fees on account is for what's being earned and that it may be appropriate to reduce fees on account. In addition, the Court stated that the local rules are very clear with respect to the investments, that the investments were made without Court approval, and that it is inclined to allow the investments that have been made and require following the local rules going forward. Ms. Cohen stated these issues were raised proactively by them in the petition, that the first year of the trust and the conservatorship administration involved a lot of work, and spoke of the Conservatee's health issues, and having to deal with the eviction issue and how hard they had to work with the Counsel to get the eviction dropped. Ms. Cohen also stated that she would like the opportunity to look at the time entry invoices (10 hours) and supplement the petition, that the Trustee endeavored that $7,500.00 was approved, that it was the Trustee's understanding that the fees on account covered both the trust administration and the conservatorship administration, that the Trustee is quite clear on this now and that a bulk of the time spent was on the conservatorship. Ms. Cohen also stated that San Francisco's view on broadened investment authority is different than that of the other Court's in the area, that it was the Trustee's understanding that ETF's fit within Probate Code 2574 authority and that she was not aware of the investments until she saw the accounting. The Amended First Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is continued to September 3, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to amend this petition to address the issues and file a petition in the conservatorship matter to request fees which will get its own hearing date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
